methods.description: This method describes the process of enriching valve silica deposition vesicles (SDVs) from T. pseudonana cell cultures using density gradient centrifugation. The procedure is performed to isolate and concentrate the SDVs for further analysis.

steps.description: A 300-mL T. pseudonana cell culture was synchronized by two consecutive silicon starvation-replenishment cycles, PDMPO labeled, and lysed as described previously 3 h after silicic acid addition.
steps.searchable_summary: synchronize and lyse T. pseudonana cell culture after silicon treatment

steps.description: The cell lysate was diluted to 30 mL with DGB, laid on top of a preformed Percoll® gradient and centrifuged for 1 h at 2,000 g.
steps.searchable_summary: centrifuge cell lysate on Percoll gradient
outputs: centrifuged gradient with visible green fraction

steps.description: The visible green fraction was collected, diluted to 30 mL with DGB and subsequently centrifuged again through a freshly prepared Percoll® gradient.
steps.searchable_summary: re-centrifuge visible green fraction on new Percoll gradient

steps.description: The visible green fraction (PF1) was collected, diluted to 25 mL with DGB, laid on top of a sucrose cushion and centrifuged at 5,000 g for 2 h.
steps.searchable_summary: centrifuge PF1 fraction on sucrose cushion

steps.description: The remaining cushion volume (SF2) was collected, diluted to 60 mL with DGB and centrifuged at 5,000 g for 16 h.
steps.searchable_summary: centrifuge SF2 fraction for extended period

steps.description: The supernatant was discarded, and the pelleted SF2 was stored at −20 °C until further use.
